popularStory.headline: Marriage to Katherine Stanley & their children
popularStory.description:
John married Katherine Stanley. They had 10 sons and 5 daughters:[1]

John K.G., K.B.
Thomas, cleric
Humphrey, Knt.
Lawrence, Knt.
James
Edward, Knt.
Christopher, Knt.
George
William
Richard, Knt.
Ell …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Savage於1422年11月1日出生在英國爾克里夫頓時，他的父親John III Savage22歲，母親Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton16歲。他於大約1447年與Katherine Stanley在英國爾克里夫頓結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1485年登記服兵役。他於1451年被列為mayor of vale royal。他去世於1495年11月22日在英國英格蘭爾普雷斯特波利馬可萊斯費爾德 科華士 煙雨 維爾德波阿可洛享年73歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾馬可萊斯費爾德聖 米可艾爾也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Savage于1422年11月1日出生在英国爾克里夫頓时，他的父亲John III Savage22岁，母亲Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton16岁。他于大约1447年与Katherine Stanley在英国爾克里夫頓结婚。他们至少是11个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1485年登记服兵役。他于1451年被列为mayor of vale royal。他去世于1495年11月22日在英国英格兰爾普雷斯特波利馬可萊斯費爾德 科華士 煙雨 維爾德波阿可洛享年73岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾馬可萊斯費爾德聖 米可艾爾也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Savage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Savage was born on 1 November 1422, in Clifton, Cheshire, England, his father, John III Savage, was 22 and his mother, Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton, was 16. He married Katherine Stanley about 1447, in Clifton, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1485. In 1451, his occupation is listed as mayor of vale royal. He died on 22 November 1495, in Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough, Prestbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73, and was buried in St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Savage est né le 1 novembre 1422, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, John III Savage, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Stanley environ 1447, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1485. En 1451, sa profession de son indiquée est mayor of vale royal. Il est décédé le 22 novembre 1495, à Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough, Prestbury, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Savage am 1. November 1422 in Clifton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John III Savage 22 und seine Mutter Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton 16. Er hat um 1447 in Clifton, Cheshire, England Katherine Stanley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 11 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1485 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1451 ist sein Beruf als mayor of vale royal aufgeführt. Er starb am 22. November 1495 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough, Prestbury, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Savage è nato il il 1 novembre 1422 nel Clifton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, John III Savage, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Katherine Stanley circa 1447, nel Clifton, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 11 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1485. Nel 1451, la sua occupazione è indicata come mayor of vale royal. Morì il 22 novembre 1495, nel Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough, Prestbury, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolto a St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Savageが1422年11月1日にイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンで生まれた時，父John III Savageは22歳，母Eleanor  Elizabeth Breretonは16歳であった。約1447年にイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンでKatherine Stanleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1485年に兵役登録をした。1451年，職業はmayor of vale royalと記録されている。彼は1495年11月22日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアプレストブリーマククレスフィールド フォーエスト ウィス ウィルドボアルクロウで73歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアマククレスフィールドスト ミチャエル'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough,: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 프레스트버이 맥클스필드 포레스트 위스 윌드보르c루그h
ko.Savage Chapel, St Michaels and All Angel: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 맥클스필드 스트 믹하얼'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: John Savage(이)가 1422년 11월 1일 영국 체실르 클리프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John III Savage(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1447년 영국 체실르 클리프턴에서 Katherine Stanley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1485년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1451년, 그의 직업은 mayor of vale royal이었다. 그는 1495년 11월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 프레스트버이 맥클스필드 포레스트 위스 윌드보르c루그h에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 맥클스필드 스트 믹하얼'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.Savage Chapel, St Michaels and All Angel: St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Savage nasceu em 1 de novembro de 1422, em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John III Savage, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Katherine Stanley aproximadamente 1447, em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 11 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1485. Em 1451, sua profissão aparece como mayor of vale royal. Ele faleceu em 22 de novembro de 1495, em Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough, Prestbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 73 anos, e foi sepultado em St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough,: макклесфиелд форест вит уаилдбоарклоую, престбури, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Savage Chapel, St Michaels and All Angel: ст мачаел'с чурч, макклесфиелд, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Savage родился 1 ноября 1422 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени John III Savage было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Stanley около 1447 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 2 дочери. В 1485 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1451 годуработал по профессии mayor of vale royal. Он умер 22 ноября 1495 г. в местности макклесфиелд форест вит уаилдбоарклоую, престбури, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности ст мачаел'с чурч, макклесфиелд, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Savage nació el 1 de noviembre de 1422, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John III Savage, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Eleanor  Elizabeth Brereton, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Katherine Stanley aproximadamente 1447, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1485. En 1451, su ocupación registrada era mayor of vale royal. Murió el 22 de noviembre de 1495, en Macclesfield Forest with Wildboarclough, Prestbury, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en St Michael's Church, Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
